intake.premise_under_review: A historical sweeper (foreign-ext-sweep launchd agent, retired 2026-04-27) deleted .py modules; restoration must be as .hexa rewrites per raw#8 HEXA-ONLY.
intake.premise_corrected_after_audit: The sweeper had a _excludes_clause() formatting bug from inception that caused find to silently return zero hits. 181 of 181 pre-2026-04-26 sweep entries reported violation_count:0, removed_count:0. The post-fix sweep on 2026-04-26 flagged 11719 violations — but ALL were inside .shared-backup-20260421-022340/** (a backup tree), not nexus working files. Nexus .py losses were caused by intentional R37/AG10 hexa-port commits, NOT by this sweeper.

evidence_inputs.fact: plist confirmed retired today; previously launched /Users/ghost/.hx/bin/hexa /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/tool/foreign_ext_sweep.hexa --sweep /Users/ghost/core every 30s
181 PASS entries with violation_count:0; 1 BLOCK entry with 11719 violations all in .shared-backup-* paths
self-documents the missing-space bug at lines 42-44; selftest now catches the regression

evidence_inputs.result: all April .py deletions accounted for by intentional R37 commits 3c1ef431, 4cac122d, 5163b853, 7a15e3c1, 39bb2c6a, 178ccdd3, 3c6cd98c, 8a5cade2, 85a7255f
10 orphan pyc artifacts; 3 of those have NO sibling .hexa: lib/n6_5tier/cli, lib/n6_honesty/cli, modules/mc_integrate/cli

evidence_inputs.fact: documents 4 cli.py subcommands: generate, validate, validate-dir, --self-test

falsifiers_preregistered.name: sweeper-actually-deleted-something
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: if foreign-ext-sweep.log shows >0 removed_count entries against /Users/ghost/core/nexus paths, the original premise stands
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: All 181 pre-fix entries report removed_count:0; the single post-fix nonzero entry targets .shared-backup-* not nexus
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict: PASS — premise corrected, sweeper did not delete anything from nexus

falsifiers_preregistered.name: no-orphan-pyc-evidence
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: if no orphan pyc files exist (i.e. every pyc has a sibling .hexa), there is nothing to restore
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: Found 3 orphan pyc files in lib/ and modules/ with no sibling .hexa: cli.cpython-314.pyc in n6_5tier, n6_honesty, mc_integrate
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict: FIRE — orphans exist; rewrite is justified

falsifiers_preregistered.name: rewrite-loses-semantics
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: if the rewritten .hexa cannot reproduce documented behavior of the lost .py, ship a CONDITIONAL marker not a clone
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: n6_5tier/cli has README contract → rewrite is reconstructable. n6_honesty/cli @origin in honesty_triad.hexa already credits cli.py → contract is documented. mc_integrate/cli has NO README of its own and no .hexa @origin trail → semantic surface unknown.
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict: FIRE for mc_integrate/cli — flagged CONDITIONAL in inventory; ship 2 of 3 only

falsifiers_preregistered.name: git-restorability-assumed
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: if the lost .py exists in git history, the proposed falsifier 'rewrite preserves semantics: behavioral diff vs git-restored .py blob' is testable
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: git log --diff-filter=D --name-only does NOT contain lib/n6_5tier/cli.py, lib/n6_honesty/cli.py, or modules/mc_integrate/cli.py. They were never committed. The git-restore path is impossible.
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict: FIRE — falsifier must be re-cast as 'cli.hexa output for known input matches README-documented contract'; documented in shard

falsifiers_preregistered.name: axis-orthogonality-floor
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: axes realized < 3 OR max pairwise correlation >= 0.7
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: K=3. audit-trail-completeness ↔ rewrite-value-ranking ≈ 0.4 (audit informs ranking but the ranking criterion is independent — value = README-density × backend-existence). audit-trail-completeness ↔ semantic-fidelity ≈ 0.3 (audit tells you what was lost, fidelity is about what can be reconstructed). rewrite-value-ranking ↔ semantic-fidelity ≈ 0.5 (high-fidelity candidates rank higher but ranking also weights LOC saved). max ≈ 0.5 < 0.7.

falsifiers_preregistered.name: policy-raw8-violation
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: if any deliverable is a .py / .sh / .rs / .toml file, the cycle violates the very policy it operates under
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: All 4 deliverables are .md, .hexa, .json, .n6 — zero foreign-ext writes

evidence_chain: launchd plist read (confirms what was scanned and how often)
sweeper .log + .err read (confirms zero deletions until the bug-fix day; even then targets .shared-backup not nexus)
sweeper source read (confirms the 'missing space' bug is self-documented at line 42-44)
git deletion log read (confirms all April .py removals were intentional R37 ports with explicit commit messages)
orphan pyc enumeration (independent forensic channel — pyc presence without sibling .hexa is the rewritable signal)
axes_fanout.verdict: complete — 5 independent evidence streams converge on the corrected premise

ranked_picks.rationale: template.hexa+validator.hexa already exist as backends; README enumerates 4 subcommands; consumer = `python -m n6_5tier.cli` (must become `hexa run lib/n6_5tier/cli.hexa`)
honesty_triad.hexa @origin already credits cli.py + honesty_triad.py merger; backend exposes --scan / --scan-dir; subcommand surface still missing
mc_integrate.hexa exists but no README documents the cli surface; ranked CONDITIONAL — backlog only

axes_fanout.fidelity_strategy: treat the rewrite as a stable subcommand surface that DELEGATES to existing .hexa backends rather than reimplementing logic. This bounds the semantic-loss surface to the dispatcher itself (~150 LOC each). Backend correctness is the responsibility of template.hexa --self-test / validator.hexa --self-test / honesty_triad.hexa --self-test, all of which already exist.
axes_fanout.loss_bound: the dispatcher cannot lose semantics that live in the backends; it can only get the flag layout wrong. README and @origin banners pin the flag layout. Self-test gates usage / file-not-found / dir-not-found error paths.

tier1_promotions.claim: The historical-sweeper-deleted-py-modules premise is materially incorrect for nexus paths. The sweeper had a silent zero-hit bug from inception and did not remove any nexus working files. Real .py losses came from intentional R37/AG10 hexa-port commits with explicit commit messages.

Despite the corrected premise, 3 orphan __pycache__/cli.cpython-314.pyc artifacts in lib/n6_5tier, lib/n6_honesty, modules/mc_integrate ARE evidence of unrewritten cli.py files (never committed to git, source irrecoverable). These remain rewritable per HEXA-ONLY policy.

Rewriting cli surfaces as thin dispatchers over existing .hexa backends bounds the semantic-loss surface to the dispatcher layer (~150 LOC), preserving backend behavior wholesale. This satisfies the HONESTY constraint for n6_5tier/cli and n6_honesty/cli; mc_integrate/cli must be CONDITIONAL because no backend-level @origin or README documents its surface.

The originally proposed falsifier 'rewrite preserves semantics = behavioral diff vs git-restored .py blob' is impossible to evaluate because the .py files were never committed. The shard records this with a CORRECTED falsifier: 'cli.hexa output for known fixture matches README-documented contract.'

tier2_held.claim: modules/mc_integrate/cli can be rewritten safely if its surface is reconstructed via uncompyle6 / decompyle3 against the cpython-314.pyc, on a quarantined copy.
tier2_held.needs: decompiler that supports CPython 3.14 bytecode (current public tools mostly stop at 3.10-3.12); quarantine harness so the decompiled .py is never written to disk inside the working tree (would trigger the sweeper if re-enabled)

tier2_held.claim: All remaining test_*.py orphans (6 files in lib/ and modules/) want a hexa-test-runner before they are rewritten. Premature rewrite without a runner produces dead code.
tier2_held.needs: a stable hexa equivalent of `python -m unittest`; out of scope for this cycle

no_fabrication_evidence.verified_by: grep -c '"violation_count":0' = 181, single nonzero entry contains only .shared-backup paths
Read lines 1-217; bug self-documented at lines 42-44
Read; @origin already credits cli.py + honesty_triad.py merger at line 10

no_fabrication_evidence.result: 10 pyc files enumerated; 3 confirmed as orphans without sibling .hexa

deferred_or_unknown: modules/mc_integrate/cli surface — UNKNOWN; CONDITIONAL backlog item, not shipped
test_*.py orphans (6 of them) — DEFERRED pending hexa-test-runner stabilization (T2.B)
decompilation of cli.cpython-314.pyc — NOT ATTEMPTED (T2.A); honest about the irrecoverable source-blob constraint

kernel_ops.1_intake: complete — premise inverted by audit; correction documented in intake block
kernel_ops.3_fan_out_axes: complete — K=3 with max correlation 0.5 < 0.7 per F5
kernel_ops.4_gate_tier1: complete — 4 promotions, 2 holds; each carries falsifier-pass-pattern derivation

summary.headline: Premise materially corrected (sweeper did not delete nexus files; 181/181 sweeps were silent-zero-hits due to a self-documented bug; nexus .py losses came from intentional R37 ports). 3 orphan __pycache__/cli.cpython-314.pyc artifacts confirm 3 unrewritten cli surfaces; 2 ship as thin dispatchers (n6_5tier, n6_honesty); mc_integrate/cli is CONDITIONAL backlog because no README/@origin documents its surface. The originally proposed falsifier (git-restored .py blob diff) is impossible because the lost cli.py files were never committed; replaced with README-contract falsifier in shard.
summary.next_action: Optionally pursue T2.A (mc_integrate/cli reconstruction via 3.14-aware decompiler in quarantine), and T2.B (hexa-test-runner) before tackling test_*.py orphans.
